channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy city-hops across Europe, promoting 'victory plan' to allies.
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was city-hopping across Europe on Thursday to promote a “victory plan” that he said “aims to create the right conditions for a just end to the war" against Russia, detailing the proposals to European leaders after a scheduled summit involving U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN-backed experts say Israel is destroying Gaza's health sector and both sides have tortured people
item.description: A U.N. commission on Thursday accused Israel of destroying Gaza's health care system through “relentless and deliberate attacks” in its yearlong war with Hamas and said that Palestinian prisoners and Israeli hostages have been tortured and sexually abused.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel continues to weigh options, timing on Iran strike following Biden-Netanyahu call
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a call that in part focused on Iran's recent missile attack on the Jewish state, and reportedly how Israel intends to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia’s river guardians battle to protect the Atrato amid threats and abandonment
item.description: PAIMADO, Colombia (AP) — Sediment and pebbles are all that’s left on the earth around much of Bernardino Mosquera’s small riverside community in northwest Colombia’s Choco region.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump-Putin ties face fresh scrutiny with Woodward book
item.description: Less than a month before the U.S. presidential election, former President Trump is facing fresh scrutiny on his relationship with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Turkey's navy starts to evacuate its citizens from Beirut as Israel battles Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Over 2,000 Turkish citizens and some foreign nationals started boarding a Turkish military ship late Wednesday that brought in aid and will take them out of a country being hit by Israeli airstrikes targeting Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from an AP investigation of Russia recruiting Africans to make drones for use in Ukraine
item.description: About 200 women ages 18-22 from across Africa have been recruited to work in a factory alongside Russian vocational students assembling thousands of Iranian-designed attack drones to be launched into Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and LORI HINNANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy will present his Ukraine 'victory plan' to Western leaders but Biden meeting is postponed
item.description: DUBROVNIK, Croatia (AP) — Ukraine's president on Wednesday said he intends to present his “victory plan” to Western leaders this week, but a weekend meeting to discuss it has been postponed as U.S. President Joe Biden said he had to stay home to respond to Hurricane Milton’ s landfall in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records reviewed by The Associated Press. According to AP, the records showed a printing company in eastern China shipped nearly 120,000 of the Trump Bibles to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID-19 tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic, as was revealed in a new book by journalist Bob Woodward, Bloomberg reported.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes another arms depot inside Russia, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian drone struck an important arms depot inside Russia, the Ukraine military said Wednesday, three weeks after another drone blasted a major Russian armory and three days after a drone smashed into a key oil terminal in Russia-occupied Crimea.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pioneers in artificial intelligence win the Nobel Prize in physics
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Two pioneers of artificial intelligence — John Hopfield and Geoffrey Hinton — won the Nobel Prize in physics Tuesday for helping create the building blocks of machine learning that is revolutionizing the way we work and live but also creates new threats for humanity.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL NIEMANN, SETH BORENSTEIN and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel defense minister cancels visit with Pentagon chief
item.description: Israel has canceled a planned Wednesday meeting between its Defense Minister Yoav Gallant and U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin, the Pentagon confirmed Tuesday. “We were just informed that Minister Gallant will be postponing his trip to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: France’s minority government survives a no-confidence vote, 2 weeks after taking office
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s minority government survived a no-confidence vote on Tuesday, two weeks after taking office, getting over the first hurdle placed by left-wing lawmakers to bring down new conservative Prime Minister Michel Barnier.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Travelers from Rwanda to be screened for Marburg
item.description: U.S. health officials are planning to screen travelers from Rwanda amid the outbreak of the Marburg virus in the country, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) announced on Monday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is set to begin scanning the week of Oct.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Former President Trump's campaign is rejecting accounts in journalist Bob Woodward's new book that the Republican presidential nominee has held multiple phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving office in January 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un pushes goal to become nuclear powerhouse in warning to South Korea
item.description: Kim Jong Un issued his second ominous message, saying he plans to make Pyongyang a top nuclear player and expand its capabilities in face of South Korea-U.S. partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican mayor's severed head placed atop pickup truck 6 days after taking office
item.description: The mayor of a violence-plagued city in Mexico was killed on Sunday with his decapitated body left in a pickup truck and his severed head placed atop the vehicle's roof.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: War rages on multiple fronts as Israel marks a year since Hamas' Oct. 7 attack
item.description: RE'IM, Israel (AP) — Israelis held somber ceremonies Monday to mark a year since the deadliest attack in the country’s history, a Hamas-led raid that shattered its sense of security and has since spiraled into wars on two fronts with no end in sight. Hamas marked the anniversary of its Oct.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mexican mayor murdered less than a week after taking office
item.description: Mayor Alejandro Arcos of Chilpancingo, Mexico, has been confirmed dead less than a week after being sworn into the notoriously dangerous city's highest office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How many are dead, missing one year since Hamas' attack on Israel?
item.description: Oct. 7 marks the first anniversary of Hamas’ attack on Israel, which prompted an Israeli invasion of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Russia jails American Stephen Hubbard over fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine
item.description: Stephen Hubbard, 72, of Michigan, has been sentenced by a Russian court Monday after being convicted of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's Churchill moment comes amid multifront war against Iranian terror, expert says
item.description: Netanyahu is facing his greatest challenge as Israel’s longest-serving Prime Minister while leading the Jewish state in an existential war against the county’s Islamist terrorist enemies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian arms dealer Viktor Bout, who was traded for Brittney Griner, to sell weapons to Iran-backed Houthis
item.description: Russian "Merchant of Death" Viktor Bout has reportedly resumed his role as an arms dealer following his December 2022 release from U.S. custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The world's rivers faced the driest year in three decades in 2023, the UN weather agency says
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. weather agency is reporting that 2023 was the driest year in more than three decades for the world's rivers, as the record-hot year underpinned a drying up of water flows and contributed to prolonged droughts in some places.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah rocket attack on Israel hits Haifa for first time
item.description: Hezbollah missiles landed in Haifa for the first time on Monday, prompting a swift response from the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: New film documents Oct. 7 attacks after first anniversary
item.description: Pierre Rehov, speaking from Ashdod, Israel, said he was disturbed by reactions that seemed to excuse the attack.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: A strike on a mosque kills at least 19, Palestinian officials say, as Israel bombards northern Gaza and southern Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New report finds nearly 200% increase in antisemitic incidents in US since Oct. 7 Hamas terror massacre
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League recorded around 10,000 antisemitic incidents since Oct. 7, marking the largest year-to-year increase since the group started recording such incidents in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli airstrikes rock southern suburbs of Beirut and cut off a key crossing into Syria
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel carried out another series of punishing airstrikes Friday, hitting suburban Beirut and cutting off the main border crossing between Lebanon and Syria for tens of thousands of people fleeing the Israeli bombardment of the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New MH370 project searches for answers on vanished plane
item.description: A new project is searching for answers about what happened to Malaysian Airlines Flight MH370 and its 239 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korea's Kim threatens to destroy South Korea with nuclear strikes if provoked
item.description: The latest comments come during heightened animosities over the North’s recent disclosure of a nuclear facility.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation
